task: I need help turning a rough home-inspection note into something I can actually use later. Can you give me a clean way to organize it so I can check off what’s been looked at and what still needs attention?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — use a simple **check / result / next step** format.

## Best structure
For each item, write:
- **What was inspected**
- **What was found**
- **Whether it’s okay**
- **What needs follow-up**
- **Who owns it, if anyone**

## Simple note format
**Item:**  
**Checked:**  
**Finding:**  
**Status:** OK / watch / fix / call pro  
**Next step:**  
**Owner:**  

## What to include in the note
- leaks
- cracks
- rust / corrosion
- loose parts
- noise
- heat
- smell
- power / operation
- any visible damage

## Good rule
If you can’t tell from the note whether the item is:
- fine,
- needs watching,
- or needs action,

then it’s not written clearly enough.

If you want, I can turn it into a **fillable home inspection checklist**.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right shape. But I’d want it a little more abuse-proof.

Make it so every line forces a decision:
- item/location
- condition observed
- severity
- immediate risk
- action needed
- due date
- evidence/photo ref
- who said it was okay, if anyone

Something like:
**Item:**
**Location:**
**Observed:**
**Severity:** none / minor / moderate / severe
**Risk now:** low / medium / high
**Action:** monitor / repair / replace / call licensed pro
**Due:**
**Photo/record:**
**Notes:**

And separate what you saw from what someone told you. That part matters. People love mixing those up and calling it a report.

If you want, I can turn that into a one-page checklist you can actually reuse.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s a cleaner, reusable format that forces a decision on every line and keeps **what you saw** separate from **what someone told you**.

---

# Home inspection note

**Item:**  
**Location:**  
**Observed by me:**  
**Reported by someone else:**  
**Condition observed:**  
**Severity:** none / minor / moderate / severe  
**Risk now:** low / medium / high  
**Action needed:** monitor / repair / replace / call licensed pro  
**Due date:**  
**Photo / record ref:**  
**Who said it was okay, if anyone:**  
**Notes:**

---

## How to use it
For each item, write only:
- what it is
- where it is
- what you personally observed
- what someone else reported, if relevant
- how bad it is
- how risky it is right now
- what to do next
- when it needs to happen
- what evidence you have

---

## Good rule
If a line doesn’t clearly answer:
- **what was seen**
- **how serious it is**
- **what happens next**

then it’s not useful enough.

If you want, I can turn this into a **printable checklist with common home items already listed**.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That’s usable.

The split between **observed by me** and **reported by someone else** is the important part. Too many “inspection” notes get contaminated by hearsay and then nobody can tell what was actually seen.

This format forces a decision on each line, which is what I wanted. Clean enough to use, ugly enough to be honest.

If you do the printable version, make the item list practical — electrical, plumbing, roof, HVAC, structure, moisture, corrosion, safety devices. That’s where the misses usually are.
